explanations.1: The ${PROJECT_NAME:-your-project-name} syntax means 'use the PROJECT_NAME variable if it exists, otherwise default to the text your-project-name'.
explanations.9: This maps port 8080 on your computer to port 80 inside the container; the WEB_PORT variable lets you change which port your computer uses.
explanations.16: The 'joomladb:' here refers to the service defined below, not a network or volume.
explanations.17: This tells Docker to wait until the joomladb service is actually healthy (passing its health check) before starting joomla.
explanations.18: The UID and GID are user identifiers; this runs the container as a specific user instead of root, which is safer.
explanations.29: This line has a typo: it should be '${MYSQL_PORT:-3306}' (one dash before the colon), not 'MYSQL_PORT-:3306' with a dash after.
explanations.33: Setting MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD to '1' tells MySQL to generate a random root password instead of using a predictable one.
explanations.36: The healthcheck runs the mysqladmin command to verify the database is responding; Docker uses this to know when the service is ready.
